# d3vsh0p

> Open source AI code orchestration — turns product specs into tested, reviewed, and merged production code using Claude agents.

Built by Chelsea Kent (@clkent). Licensed under BSL 1.1.

## What This Repo Is

d3vsh0p orchestrates Claude agents to build software from a structured roadmap. Riley (PM agent) creates specs and a roadmap from your idea. Morgan (principal engineer) runs as a persistent Claude Code CLI session, reads the full roadmap, implements items sequentially with complete project context, and delegates to sub-agents when parallelism helps. All work produces real commits, real PRs, and real production code.

## Project Structure

```
platform/orchestrator/          — The brain. Node.js orchestrator that drives everything
  src/index.js                  — CLI entry point (devshop command)
  src/commands/                 — CLI commands: kickoff, run, talk, pair, status, etc.
  src/commands/run.js           — Spawns Morgan CLI for roadmap execution
  src/commands/cli-spawn.js     — Shared Claude CLI spawn utilities
  src/agents/                   — Agent runner, session, and template engine
  src/git/                      — Git operations, merge, consolidation
  src/quality/                  — Health checks, convention checks, review parsing
  src/roadmap/                  — Roadmap reader/writer, format checkers, OpenSpec reader
  src/runners/                  — Repair, security, scaffolding
  src/session/                  — Cost tracking, session utils, path utils
  src/scheduler/                — launchd/cron integration for automated runs

templates/agents/               — System prompt templates for each agent role
  pm-agent/                     — Riley — creates specs and roadmaps via interactive Q&A
  principal-engineer/           — Morgan — orchestrates runs, reviews, repairs, pairs
  security-agent/               — Casey — vulnerability scanning
  triage-agent/                 — Drew — classifies parked items
  _shared/                      — Shared prompt partials (roadmap rules, delegation, etc.)

openspec/                       — d3vsh0p's own specs (it uses OpenSpec on itself)
active-agents/                  — Per-project runtime state and session logs
templates/project-starter/      — Starter code copied when creating new projects
```

## Key Concepts

### Two Agents, One Workflow
- **Riley (PM)** — Creates OpenSpec specs and a phased roadmap from your idea through interactive Q&A
- **Morgan (Principal Engineer)** — Persistent CLI session that reads the roadmap, implements items, runs tests, commits, and marks progress. Delegates to sub-agents for parallel groups with scoped briefs.

### Phases and Groups
The roadmap organizes work into phases (sequential, with dependencies) containing groups (independent work). Morgan works through phases in order, implementing items or delegating groups to sub-agents when parallelism helps.

### CLI Sessions
All interactive commands (kickoff, run, talk, pair) spawn Claude Code CLI sessions with `stdio: 'inherit'`. The user interacts directly with the agent. Sessions can be resumed with `--resume`.

### Git Strategy
- Morgan commits directly on the current branch during runs
- Session branches auto-consolidate to main via PR when Morgan finishes
- Sub-agents use worktree isolation via Claude Code's Agent tool

## Key Files to Read First

1. `platform/orchestrator/src/commands/run.js` — Entry point for `./devshop run`, spawns Morgan CLI
2. `templates/agents/principal-engineer/run-prompt.md` — Morgan's orchestration instructions
3. `templates/agents/_shared/roadmap-execution-rules.md` — How Morgan works through the roadmap
4. `templates/agents/_shared/sub-agent-delegation.md` — When and how Morgan delegates
5. `platform/orchestrator/src/commands/cli-spawn.js` — Shared CLI spawn utilities
6. `platform/orchestrator/src/commands/kickoff.js` — Project creation flow with Riley
7. `platform/orchestrator/src/roadmap/roadmap-reader.js` — Roadmap parsing and item marking
8. `platform/orchestrator/src/agents/template-engine.js` — How system prompts are rendered
9. `CLAUDE.md` — Instructions for AI assistants working on this codebase

## How to Modify Agent Behavior

Agent prompts live in `templates/agents/<role>/`. Each has a system prompt template with Handlebars-style variables (`{{PROJECT_ID}}`, `{{ROADMAP_CONTENT}}`, etc.) that the template engine fills at runtime. Shared partials in `_shared/` are included via `{{>partial-name}}`. To change what an agent does, edit its prompt template.

## How to Add a New Command

1. Create `platform/orchestrator/src/commands/<name>.js`
2. Export an async function that receives the parsed CLI args
3. Register it in `platform/orchestrator/src/index.js`
4. Follow the OpenSpec workflow for non-trivial changes (see CLAUDE.md)

## Testing

```bash
cd platform/orchestrator && npm test
```

Tests are colocated with source files (`*.test.js`). A pre-commit hook runs tests automatically when orchestrator files change.
